title: Empirical Concept Observation Architecture (ECOA) — Canonical Schema
description: ECOA is the observational realization architecture of DALE. Its role is to transform admissible abstract system observations into structured empirical observation states while preserving observational admissibility, structural coherence, traceability, readable reconstruction, and architectural continuity. ECOA operationalizes observation — it does not redefine DALE, replace DALE, or function independently from DALE foundational architecture.

architectural_position.note: ECOA is not a tool. It is the observational realization architecture that tools operate within.
architectural_position.position_in_dale: DALE → ECOA → Observable System Realization → Readable Operational Structures → Tool Layer
architectural_position.canonical_law: DALE foundational architecture remains invariant. ECOA observational realization may adapt only within the admissible observational boundary defined by DALE.

structural_continuity_law.statement: Every observational layer must remain recoverable from the layer beneath it.
recovery_chain: Readable Return — recoverable from Operational Reconstruction
Operational Reconstruction — recoverable from Empirical Assignment
Empirical Assignment — recoverable from Variable Realization
Variable Realization — recoverable from Recursive Decomposition
Recursive Decomposition — recoverable from Observation Formation

traceability_law.statement: Every ECOA observational output must remain recoverable from the observational structures, decomposition pathways, assignment states, and reconstruction layers that generated it.

adaptation_law.statement: Observable realization may adapt. Foundational observational architecture may not.

integration_law.statement: Tools may expose, organize, store, and operationalize ECOA realization, but may not replace, redefine, or obscure the architectural continuity that generates observational outputs.

governance_law.statement: Observational realizations may evolve only through traceable, structurally validated, canon-governed adaptation that preserves foundational DALE architectural continuity.

variable_architecture_law.statement: Variables remain structurally dependent realization layers through which admissible observations become empirically assignable and operationally reconstructible within traceable ECOA architecture.

reconstruction_law.statement: Readable operational return must remain reconstructible from empirical realization through traceable reconstruction continuity across all observational layers.

structural_status_law.statement: ECOA must preserve explicit distinguishability between observational realization, informational absence, structural absence, inactivity, non-observability, and balance-relevant missingness across all observational layers.

invariant_layers.description: These layers constitute protected DALE foundational architecture. They may not be modified during ordinary implementation, tool realization, or client adaptation.

adaptable_layers.description: These layers may adapt within canon-governed architectural constraints.

ObservationPackage.description: Structured transfer package preserving observational continuity. Observations must enter ECOA through structured observation packages.

ObservationCondition.description: The active contextual condition under which realization occurs. Observation conditions influence activation pathways, empirical realizability, and reconstruction behavior. They do not redefine foundational architecture.

StructuralStatus.description: Canonical structural statuses. These distinctions must remain explicit throughout realization and must not collapse into neutral values or silent omissions. assigned=empirical realization exists | informational_absence=information unavailable | structural_absence=structure does not exist | structural_non_observability=structure cannot currently be observed | inactive=admissible but not activated | balance_relevant_missingness=missingness materially affects observational integrity

FundamentalVariable.description: A lower-level observational realization structure supporting empirical assignment, decomposition realization, signed-state continuity, and reconstruction dependence. Variables remain principle-dependent, model-dependent, decomposition-dependent, reconstruction-dependent, and observation-dependent.

assignment_value.description: Signed empirical state in [-1, 1] or null for non-assigned statuses. Neither extreme is a universal good/bad label.

OperationalVariable.description: A readable reconstructed operational observation structure. Operational variables are not detached labels, arbitrary reporting categories, or UI abstractions. They remain reconstructible from lower-level realization structures.

FamilyStructure.description: Organizational grouping of operational variables preserving readable coherence. Family organization must remain reconstruction-grounded, not presentation-driven.

VariableActivationRecord.description: Preserves variable participation. Variable activation depends on observation condition, decomposition pathway, realizability, structural relevance, and admissible visibility. Inactive variables remain distinct from absent variables.

AssignmentRecord.description: Preserves empirical assignment continuity. Assignment occurs only after admissible realization, decomposition continuity, and variable activation. Assignment does not prescribe action, optimize systems, rank outcomes, or generate normative evaluation.

StructuralStatusRecord.description: Preserves missingness visibility. These statuses must not collapse into neutral values or silent omissions.

balance_impact.description: True if this status materially affects observational integrity

ReconstructionPath.description: Preserves reconstruction continuity. Readable outputs may not detach from realization architecture. Readable interpretation without recoverability is structurally invalid.

ReadableReturnRecord.description: Preserves readable output continuity. The final observational output layer of ECOA. Must remain architecture-dependent, observation-dependent, and traceability-bound.

AdaptationRecord.description: Preserves realization evolution continuity. Adapted architectures become official ECOA realizations only after canonization.

canonical_observation_flow.description: The canonical ECOA observation flow. This sequence remains invariant across observational realizations.

observation_flows.observational_realization_flow: Observation Formation → Decomposition → Variable Activation → Assignment → Reconstruction → Readable Return
observation_flows.variable_realization_flow: Decomposition → Terminal Realization → Variable Activation → Assignment
observation_flows.reconstruction_flow: Assignment → Operational Reconstruction → Family Organization → Readable Return
observation_flows.traceability_flow: Observation Condition → Variable Activation → Assignment → Reconstruction → Trace Path
observation_flows.adaptation_flow: Observation Need → Variable Design → Validation → Canonization
observation_flows.integration_flow: Observation Package → ECOA Realization → Readable Return → Tool Exposure

reconstruction: Flattened scoring systems — collapse structural states
KPI substitution — detaches from realization architecture
governance: Experimental realization treated as canonical without review
